_meta.author: Claude (scorecard-refine-local autopilot)
_meta.note: Fort Worth, TX City Council batch — evidence-based finalization. Rosters web-verified June 2026. Carlos Flores (D2), Charlie Lauersdorf (D4), Chris Nettles (D8), Deborah Peoples (D5) confirmed current after the May 3 2025 election. Alan Blaylock VACATED Council Dist. 10 to run for Texas House Dist. 93 (won the Mar 3 2026 GOP primary, now the Nov 2026 nominee); Chris Jamieson won the May 2026 special election to fill his unexpired D10 term — so Blaylock is reframed to a STATE candidate record. Scores reset to null where no evidence; FALSE only on a documented contrary vote/statement/endorsement; TRUE only on documented alignment. Nettles party corrected R->D (Our Revolution endorsement); FW council seats are officially nonpartisan.

set.office: Texas House District 93 (2026 Republican nominee; former Fort Worth Dist. 10 councilmember)

profile.education: Tarrant County College; Texas Christian University (TCU)
profile.background: Oil & gas / product-management career (Nokia, Core Laboratories/Owen Oil Tools). Fort Worth City Council Dist. 4 (2022-2023) then Dist. 10 (2023-2026), chaired the Audit & Finance Committee; vacated the seat to run for Texas House Dist. 93.

alan-blaylock.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster web-verified: Blaylock is NO LONGER on Fort Worth City Council. He was re-elected to Dist. 10 unopposed in May 2025, then resigned to run for Texas House Dist. 93, won the Mar 3 2026 Republican primary (def. Steve Sprowls) and is the Nov 3 2026 nominee; Chris Jamieson won the May 2026 special election (1,836-1,761 over Alicia Ortiz) to serve the unexpired D10 term. Record reframed to the state-house race he is now in. HARD evidence (his iVoterGuide HD93 questionnaire + council record): affirms life begins at conception and 'elective abortion should never be allowed under any circumstances' incl. enforcing the Comstock Act (life q0/q1 TRUE); 'protection from conception until natural death' (life q3 TRUE, opposes euthanasia). Affirms marriage as one man + one woman, that biological sex is immutable, and child gender transition is 'child abuse' (marriage q0/q2 TRUE); as a councilman he was one of four who declined the 2024 Fort Worth Pride proclamation and publicly criticized the city's LGBTQ advisory committee as a 'harmful distraction' (marriage q4 TRUE). Supports parental right to direct a child's health/education and opposes child transition (family q1 TRUE). Participated in/ supported prayer initiatives at City Hall with faith leaders (christian_liberty q3 TRUE, SOFT). Fiscal hawk: the SOLE 'no' vote against the FY2025-26 budget/tax rate for being above the no-new-revenue rate, signed the council no-tax-increase pledge, campaigns on permanent structural property-tax cuts (economic q2 TRUE). Strongly supports photo voter ID (election q1 TRUE). Affirmatively pro-police: endorsed by the Fort Worth Police Officers Association and Tarrant County Law Enforcement Association, funded 80+ public-safety positions (public_justice q0 TRUE). Strongly opposes sanctuary cities (border q2 TRUE). Endorsed by Texas Gun Rights; opposes gun restrictions beyond basic background checks (self_defense q1 TRUE). NO EVIDENCE found on IVF/stem-cell, abortion-PAC funding, no-fault divorce, school-choice/curriculum specifics, CBDC, paper ballots/mail voting, bail/prosecutors, 10th-Amendment nullification, E-Verify/birthright/barrier specifics, constitutional carry, or NFA/castle-doctrine — those cells null.

0.note: iVoterGuide HD93: states life begins at conception and deserves legal protection from conception.

1.note: 'Elective abortion should never be allowed under any circumstances'; supports enforcing the Comstock Act on abortion drugs — an abolitionist (not merely restrictionist) position.

3.note: Affirms legal protection 'until natural death' — opposes euthanasia/quality-of-life rationing.

0.note: 'Marriage is a God-ordained, sacred and legal union of one man and one woman.'

2.note: Affirms immutable biological sex ('biological males should not occupy biological women's spaces'); calls child transition 'child abuse.'

4.note: One of four members who declined to sign the 2024 Fort Worth Pride proclamation; publicly called the city's LGBTQ advisory committee a 'harmful distraction.'

1.note: Supports parents' right to direct a child's health and education; opposes child gender transition.

3.note: SOFT: joined Mayor Parker, Judge O'Hare and faith leaders backing prayer initiatives at City Hall (Rep. Schatzline/Mercy Culture effort).

2.note: Sole 'no' vote against the FY2025-26 budget/tax rate for exceeding the no-new-revenue rate; signed the no-tax-increase pledge; campaigns on permanent structural property-tax cuts.

1.note: Strongly disagrees voters should be able to vote without photo ID — supports photo voter ID.

0.note: Endorsed by the Fort Worth Police Officers Association and Tarrant County Law Enforcement Association; funded 80+ public-safety positions; vocal pro-police.

2.note: Strongly disagrees with sanctuary-city protections for undocumented persons.

1.note: Opposes gun restrictions beyond 'basic background checks'; on the Texas Gun Rights 2026 endorsement ballot.

profile.religion: Catholic — All Saints Catholic Church parish; Nolan Catholic High School alum
profile.education: UT Austin (Aerospace Engineering); degree completed at UT Arlington
profile.background: Aerospace stress engineer (Lockheed Martin F-16/F-35). Third-generation Northside Fort Worth; former Zoning Commission chair. City Council Dist. 2 since 2017, re-elected May 3 2025 (term to 2027).

carlos-flores.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster web-verified: incumbent Dist. 2, won the May 3 2025 general outright (def. Joe Ponce Jr.), term to 2027; FW council is officially nonpartisan. HARD evidence: affirmatively pro-police beyond budget votes — endorsed by the Fort Worth Police Officers Association, sits on the Crime Control & Prevention District board, and drove police capacity (NW Division bike patrol, larger academy classes, lateral hires) (public_justice q0 TRUE). In June 2024 he supported certificates of recognition for LGBTQ organizations after the Pride proclamation failed (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE). In Aug 2025 he publicly opposed a Fort Worth-ICE (287g) partnership over community-trust concerns (border q1/q2 FALSE). He declined every faith/family item on the iVoterGuide questionnaire. He voted for budgets that lowered the property-tax rate (8 of last 10 yrs), but total spending rose with fee hikes, so that routine governance is left null on economic q2. NO EVIDENCE found on abortion, marriage definition, schools, Christian liberty, elections, or guns — null.

4.note: June 2024: supported certificates of recognition for LGBTQ organizations after the Pride proclamation failed — does not oppose LGBTQ promotion in city policy. (He did not back the separate LGBTQ advisory committee.)

0.note: Endorsed by the Fort Worth Police Officers Association; serves on the Crime Control & Prevention District board; affirmatively expanded police capacity (bike patrol, academy classes, lateral hires).

1.note: Aug 2025: said he would not support a Fort Worth-ICE partnership, citing harm to public trust — does not support mandatory deportation/ICE enforcement.

2.note: Opposes city cooperation with ICE (287g) — a sanctuary-leaning position.

profile.education: B.A. Intelligence Studies, American Military University; Executive MBA, TCU
profile.background: U.S. Marine Corps (First Sergeant, Reserve; ~20 yrs, multiple deployments, wounded 2004). Small-business owner (real-estate media). City Council Dist. 4 since 2023, re-elected May 2025 (~74%), chairs the Public Safety Committee (term to 2027).

charlie-lauersdorf.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster web-verified: incumbent Dist. 4, re-elected May 3 2025 (~74% over Teresa Ramirez Gonzalez), term to 2027; FW council is officially nonpartisan. HARD evidence: fiscal hawk — one of three members to vote AGAINST the FY2025 tax rate as insufficient relief, doubled the senior/disabled homestead exemption, pushed below the no-new-revenue rate, calls rising property taxes 'unsustainable' (economic q2 TRUE). Affirmatively pro-police: chairs the Public Safety Committee, self-describes as 'one of the most pro-police members,' speaks at every Police Academy graduation, fought to keep officer take-home vehicles, added 106 police positions (public_justice q0 TRUE). DEVIATION/NUANCE: he was one of four who declined the 2024 Pride proclamation, BUT explicitly said he would '100% support' a general Pride proclamation and objected only to it naming specific organizations — so his marriage q4 stance is genuinely mixed and left NULL, not scored. Separately, in June 2025 he opposed the city's DEI department and minority/women-business incentives ('virtue signaling') — noted but no clean local rubric cell. NO EVIDENCE found on life, marriage definition, schools, Christian liberty, elections, immigration, or guns — null.

2.note: One of three to vote against the FY2025 tax rate as too high; doubled the senior/disabled exemption and pushed below the no-new-revenue rate; calls property taxes 'unsustainable.'

0.note: Chairs the Public Safety Committee; self-described 'most pro-police' member; speaks at every Police Academy graduation; fought for officer take-home vehicles and added 106 police positions.

profile.religion: Senior Pastor, Purpose Driven Ministries (Baptist), Fort Worth
profile.background: Baptist pastor and childcare/afterschool business owner (Haven of Purpose); former Tarrant County administrative clerk. City Council Dist. 8 since 2021, re-elected May 2025 (~74%), term to 2027.

chris-nettles.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster web-verified: incumbent Dist. 8, re-elected May 3 2025 (~74%), term to 2027. PARTY CORRECTED R->D: FW council is officially nonpartisan, but Nettles is Democratic/progressive-aligned (endorsed by Bernie Sanders' Our Revolution) — the prior 'R' was a data error. HARD evidence: supported the 2024 Fort Worth Pride proclamation and presented recognition certificates to LGBTQ organizations, accusing dissenting colleagues of wanting to 'control the narrative' (biblical_marriage q4 FALSE). He is the council's leading proponent of a citizen/community police OVERSIGHT review board (sponsored proposal, failed 5-4), framed around accountability — the opposite of affirmatively backing law enforcement (public_justice q0 FALSE). Opposes a Fort Worth-ICE (287g) partnership (border q1/q2 FALSE). Notable: an explicitly Christian pastor whose faith has NOT translated into any pro-life, traditional-marriage, or religious-liberty advocacy on record. His gun-violence work targets illegal sales but no documented red-flag/AWB position, so self_defense left null. NO EVIDENCE on abortion, marriage definition, schools, Christian-liberty cells, elections, or budget posture beyond routine votes — null.

4.note: Supported the 2024 Pride proclamation and presented recognition certificates to LGBTQ organizations; criticized colleagues who declined.

0.note: Leading champion of a citizen police-oversight review board (sponsored, failed 5-4) over police objections — accountability framing, not affirmative pro-police backing.

1.note: Opposes the federal 287(g) ICE partnership; would not support forcing city departments into immigration enforcement.

2.note: Opposes city cooperation with ICE — a sanctuary-leaning position.

profile.education: B.S. and MBA, Texas Woman's University
profile.background: Retired AT&T vice president. Former Tarrant County Democratic Party chair (~2015-2023); two-time Fort Worth mayoral runner-up (2019, 2021) and 2022 county-judge nominee. City Council Dist. 5 (east Fort Worth) since May 13 2025, term to 2027.

deborah-peoples.notes_set: [2026-06 RESOLUTE refinement] Roster web-verified: won the Dist. 5 (east Fort Worth) seat OUTRIGHT on May 3 2025 (54.28%, no runoff), sworn in May 13 2025, term to 2027; succeeded retiring Gyna Bivens. HARD evidence: pro-choice — endorsed by EMILY's List (2021) and spoke at a post-Dobbs pro-choice rally urging supporters to 'recommit' (sanctity_of_life q0/q1 FALSE). On policing she said in 2021 the city should consider 'reprioritizing funding to the police, or defund the police' and moving dollars away from 'militarizing the police'; the Fort Worth Police Officers Association endorsed her opponent (public_justice q0 FALSE) — she later reframed this as offloading non-police duties and called the 'defund' label a 'dog whistle,' and has family in law enforcement (recorded for fairness). DEVIATION worth noting: she campaigns explicitly on LOWERING property taxes and cutting 'wasteful spending,' and voted for the FY2026 budget that cut the tax rate — but that is routine governance, left null on economic q2. As former party chair she defended Crystal Mason and fought a ballot-harvesting allegation as 'voter suppression' — an access posture, but no direct voter-ID statement, so election cells left null. FAITH: unknown — the 'Our Mother of Mercy' link in search was a DIFFERENT person (La'Wonda Peoples) and is not attributed. NO EVIDENCE on LGBTQ specifics, schools, Christian liberty, immigration, or guns — null.

0.note: Pro-choice: EMILY's List endorsement (a PAC backing only pro-choice candidates) and spoke at a post-Dobbs pro-choice rally — does not affirm life at conception.

1.note: Publicly pro-choice; opposes (not advocates) abortion abolition.

0.note: 2021: said the city should consider 'reprioritizing funding to the police, or defund the police'; FW Police Officers Association endorsed her opponent. Later reframed as offloading non-police duties (recorded for fairness) — not affirmative pro-police backing.
